disclaimer: Certification database lookup confirms a number exists and may show grading images — it does not by itself prove the physical slab in your hand is genuine. Counterfeiters sometimes reuse real cert numbers on fake holders.

checks.title: Label typography
checks.description: Font weight, kerning, and capitalization on the front label. PSA uses a specific proprietary font. Suspect if characters look thin, oddly spaced, or pixelated.

referenceImages.alt: PSA slab with sharp label typography
referenceImages.caption: Authentic PSA label type is crisp, evenly kerned, and uses the correct proprietary weight.

referenceImages.alt: Suspect PSA label called out in hobby authentication guide
referenceImages.caption: Counterfeits often fail on font weight, digit shapes (0/8), and GEM MT red shade.

checks.title: Silver logo placement
checks.description: Front label has a silver PSA logo at bottom center connecting to the red border. Back label has silver PSA logo at top center. Check positions are correct.

referenceImages.alt: PSA label showing silver logo position on front label
referenceImages.caption: Verify logo sits at the correct border junction—not floating or off-center.

referenceImages.alt: Diagram of PSA silver logo placement on front and back labels
referenceImages.caption: Front: silver PSA logo bottom-center on red border. Back: silver PSA logo top-center.

checks.title: Silver logo illumination
checks.description: Real silver PSA logos have an "on/off" reflective illumination effect — one side is lighter, the other darker depending on angle. In video, note if this shifting is present or absent.

referenceImages.alt: PSA Lighthouse logo illumination under tilted light
referenceImages.caption: Real Lighthouse logos shift light/dark (on/off) as you change viewing angle.

referenceImages.alt: Example of flat or incorrect PSA logo reflectivity on suspect slab
referenceImages.caption: Fakes may look uniformly shiny without the true on/off illumination effect.

checks.title: Raised plastic logo
checks.description: Real PSA slabs have a tactile raised "PSA" embossed in the bottom right corner of the plastic on both front and back. Check for its presence and correct position.

referenceImages.alt: Embossed PSA logo on case plastic corner
referenceImages.caption: Community check: tactile raised PSA on bottom-right case plastic (front and back). Not listed on PSA’s current security summary page.

referenceImages.alt: Suspect slab missing or incorrect raised PSA on case
referenceImages.caption: Missing, shallow, or mis-placed embossed PSA on the holder is a common fake tell.

checks.title: Fugitive ink watermark
checks.description: PSA labels contain a subtle blue "fugitive ink" watermark of the PSA logo on the white portions of both front and back labels. Look for a faint bluish tint or PSA outline in white label areas.

referenceImages.alt: Magnified authentic PSA label showing uniform fugitive ink pattern
referenceImages.caption: Authentic labels show subtle, even fugitive-ink / watermark pattern in white areas.

referenceImages.alt: Suspect PSA label with blotchy or intrusive watermark printing
referenceImages.caption: Counterfeit labels may show bold, irregular dots or an overly obvious PSA watermark.

checks.title: Plastic clarity & weld
checks.description: Real PSA plastic is optically clear and smooth, with no clouding, frosting, or cracking especially along the sonic weld seam. Frosting or cracks near the edges strongly indicate tampering or a fake.

referenceImages.alt: Clear PSA case plastic without clouding
referenceImages.caption: Authentic PSA plastic is optically clear with a smooth sonic weld.

referenceImages.alt: Frosting or fractures along PSA sonic weld seam
referenceImages.caption: Frosting, clouding, or cracks near the weld strongly suggest tampering or a fake holder.
referenceImages.sourceTitle: Ball Card Genius — PSA weld reference

checks.title: Serial / cert format
checks.description: PSA cert numbers are 7–8 digits. Note the serial visible on the label and verify it against PSA’s database.

referenceImages.alt: Diagram showing 7–8 digit PSA cert number location on label
referenceImages.caption: PSA cert numbers are 7–8 digits on the label. Always verify at psacard.com/cert and compare on-file images.

referenceImages.alt: Example of verifying PSA cert data against the physical card
referenceImages.caption: A valid cert number on a fake label is common—match grade, card identity, and SecureScan images.
referenceImages.sourceTitle: Ball Card Genius — fake PSA slabs

checks.title: Card-to-slab fit
checks.description: The card inside should fit snugly with uniform, minimal gap all around — not floating loosely.

referenceImages.alt: Card seated snugly inside PSA slab
referenceImages.caption: The card should have uniform, minimal gap—not floating or rattling.

referenceImages.alt: Loose or poorly seated card inside suspect holder
referenceImages.caption: Excessive gap or misalignment can indicate reholdering or counterfeit cases.

checks.title: Label color quality
checks.description: The red border on PSA labels should be a deep red, not pink or brown. Label background should be bright white, not off-yellow.

referenceImages.alt: Deep red PSA border and bright white label background
referenceImages.caption: Brick-red border and clean white label stock—not pink, brown, or yellowed.

referenceImages.alt: Suspect PSA label color and print quality
referenceImages.caption: Off-color red bars and washed label backgrounds are frequent counterfeit tells.
referenceImages.sourceTitle: Ball Card Genius — fake PSA label header

CARD_SLAB_FIT.title: Poke Master Center — fit and tampering

LABEL_COLOR_QUALITY.title: PokéSats — red shade on GEM MT bar
PSA Security: A Buyer’s Guide (label light test)
LABEL_COLOR_QUALITY.note: Hold label to strong light — authentic labels allow faint see-through; stacked fake labels do not.

checks.title: Beckett ® trademark
checks.description: "BECKETT" on the case must have a registered trademark symbol ® (not ©, not missing). The ® should be small, raised, and match the font weight.

referenceImages.alt: Beckett holder showing embedded security and branding
referenceImages.caption: Look for the registered ® on BECKETT branding on the case—not © or a missing symbol.

checks.title: Label color & size
checks.description: BGS labels are gold-colored with specific proportions. The label should not be washed out, too small, or the wrong shade of gold.

referenceImages.alt: BGS gold label proportions on graded slab
referenceImages.caption: Gold label should be correct shade and proportion—not washed out or undersized.

checks.title: Subgrade layout
checks.description: BGS labels show 4 subgrades (Centering, Corners, Edges, Surface) in specific positions with their values — check that layout matches official BGS format with correct labels.

referenceImages.alt: Diagram of BGS subgrade grid layout
referenceImages.caption: Four subgrades (Centering, Corners, Edges, Surface) must match official BGS positions.

checks.title: Inner sleeve
checks.description: BGS cards are housed inside a thin inner sleeve within the plastic case. Visible gap or direct card-to-plastic contact is suspicious.

referenceImages.alt: Beckett slab diagram showing archival inner sleeve
referenceImages.caption: Official Beckett slabs use an inner sleeve to limit card movement.
referenceImages.sourceTitle: Beckett — Built to last slab diagram

referenceImages.alt: Cross-section diagram of BGS inner sleeve inside ultrasonic weld case
referenceImages.caption: BGS cards sit in a thin inner sleeve before welding—direct card-to-plastic contact is suspicious.

checks.title: Cert number format
checks.description: BGS cert numbers are 10 digits, zero-padded (e.g. 0015876182). Verify format if visible.

referenceImages.alt: Diagram of 10-digit zero-padded BGS cert number format
referenceImages.caption: BGS cert numbers are 10 digits, zero-padded (e.g. 0015876182). Verify at Beckett card lookup.

checks.title: Plastic clarity
checks.description: BGS cases are clear with no frosting or cracks.

referenceImages.alt: Clear Beckett sonic-welded holder
referenceImages.caption: Case should be crystal clear without frosting or cracks along the weld.

checks.title: Card-to-slab fit
checks.description: Card should fit snugly, not floating.

referenceImages.alt: Grader inspecting BGS slab fit and alignment
referenceImages.caption: Loose labels or shifting cards inside the holder are red flags—BGS labels should not float.

certLinks.title: Beckett Graded Card Lookup
certLinks.note: Instant cert verification for BGS, BVG, and BCCG.

checks.title: Label typography
checks.description: Font quality, spacing, and professional print quality.

referenceImages.alt: CGC certification label with crisp typography
referenceImages.caption: CGC labels should have clean, sharp text without pixilation.

referenceImages.alt: CGC official comparison of counterfeit vs authentic print on Jordan rookie
referenceImages.caption: CGC’s counterfeit alert shows pixilated name plates and print flaws on fakes.

checks.title: Plastic clarity
checks.description: Clear plastic with no frosting, warping, or cracks.

referenceImages.alt: Clear CGC holder showing card detail
referenceImages.caption: CGC holders are ultrasonically welded and optically clear when authentic.

checks.title: Card-to-slab fit
checks.description: Card fits snugly, not floating loosely.

referenceImages.alt: CGC stackable holders with uniform fit
referenceImages.caption: Cards should be snug with intact weld—no gaps suggesting reopening.

checks.title: Serial / cert format
checks.description: Cert number is clearly printed and legible.

referenceImages.alt: CGC unique certification number on label
referenceImages.caption: Enter cert number or scan QR at cgccards.com/certlookup.

checks.title: Seal integrity
checks.description: Case weld/seal appears intact with no signs of opening.

referenceImages.alt: CGC graded Jordan rookie used in official authentication article
referenceImages.caption: Compare holder, label, and on-file images—tampered welds or swapped cards fail cert alignment.

certLinks.note: Cert number or QR scan; view holder images from grading.
